raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> I thought I was booking a round-trip flight from XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX to XXXX, XXXX. I was using the XXXX XXXX website ( this is a fairly routine trip for me, I reserve it several times a year ). When I attempted to add a rental car to the reservation I was somehow ( unknowingly ) directed to another website " XXXX XXXX  '' which turns out to be XXXX. You will note on the attached documents that " XXXX XXXX  '' uses many of XXXX XXXX logos and for all intents and purposes appears to be the same company as XXXX. After I booked the reservation on XXXX XXXX I realized that I had been misled to this website and that other, more desirable travel times and rental car options were available on the XXXX website. I IMMEDIATELY cancelled the reservation on XXXX and assumed that a refund would be coming to my AmEx credit card. After several days, when the refund did not appear, I called XXXX and was informed that the airfare portion of the reservation would be refunded but the rental car portion could not be refunded. I have not received the airline portion of the refund and I strongly disagree with the failure to issue a refund for the car. This is a VERY deceptive practice by XXXX. 

The first five pages of the attachment are the receipt for the trip and the following pages are AmEx 's explanation why the refund can not be processed. I honestly don't believe a real person at AmEx reviewed this complaint. As stated above, I disagree with everything XXXX is doing. I won't be doing business with either of these companies in the future. I would truly like to see CFPB 's involvement in this deceptive practice and I look forward to receiving your response.
